speeches.name: Alberto Musalem
speeches.summary: St. Louis Fed President Alberto Musalem supported keeping the policy rate at 3.5%-3.75%, viewing it as appropriately balanced for the dual mandate amid risks of persistent above-target inflation due to geopolitical factors and tariffs.

speeches.name: Michael S. Barr
speeches.summary: Governor Michael S. Barr supported the FOMC's decision to maintain current monetary policy, stressing vigilance against inflation persistence from recent shocks while noting economic resilience.

speeches.name: Susan M. Collins
speeches.summary: Boston Fed President Susan M. Collins delivered observations on the economic outlook and small businesses, with no detailed monetary policy stance specified in available information.

speeches.name: Beth M. Hammack
speeches.summary: Cleveland Fed President Beth M. Hammack supported the FOMC's decision to keep the federal funds rate at 3.5%-3.75%, believing it is near neutral and appropriately positioned to observe economic developments.

speeches.name: Jerome Powell
speeches.summary: Chair Jerome Powell announced the FOMC's decision to maintain the federal funds rate at 3.5%-3.75% after prior cuts, stating it is within neutral range to stabilize the labor market and allow inflation to trend toward 2%.

macro_fred.narrative: Leaning Neutral: CPI at 2.7%, Fed Funds at 3.64%, unemployment at 4.3%.
